title: Vibrant Filipino Perilla Seed Stew
description: Tangy aerated hangover cure stew featuring perilla seed deulkkae, reminiscent of classic pinakbet but with a bright citrus twist.
instructions: Heat a dry pan over medium heat and toast the perilla seeds deulkkae until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
In a large pot, sauté the onion, garlic, and ginger in a splash of oil until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the diced sweet potato, eggplant, and zucchini to the pot and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to maintain a steady simmer.
Add the green beans, toasted perilla seeds, soy sauce, lime juice, and lime zest, stirring to combine.
Simmer the stew uncovered for 15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and flavors meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
Remove from heat and stir in chopped fresh cilantro.
Serve the stew hot, optionally accompanied by steamed rice.
